widgetData.source_sentence: How do local Italian tour guides craft a narrative that connects ancient heritage with modern developments?
sentences: what is perspective? how do we draw within it? and what kind of perspectives are there? well, i'm about to show you through some of the diagrams overlaid atop of real-life examples, starting with... one-point perspective with a single vanishing point. two-point perspective with vertical lines remaining parallel to each other, plus an additional vanishing point. three-point perspective with another vanishing point below or above. 4-point perspective connected with the top and bottom points in a warped way and 5-point perspective that is quite similar to 4-point perspective and when looked at a diagram would look something like this. but while knowing these 5 linear perspectives is all well and good, you usually don't use those grids every single time to draw in perspective. so, instead i want to focus on 3 key aspects of perspective drawing, starting with proximity. now, let's look at a cube, which i'm drawing in an orthogonal way, meaning that the vanishing points of the subject are so far out of bounds of our working area that the vanishing lines connecting to those dots are parallel to each other. we might even be able to see that sort of view in a real life when looking at an object from a distance. but if we gradually increase our proximity or shorten the distance between us and the subject matter, you'll notice that the vanishing points of our subject enter

while they do serve a little bit of everything, i really recommend getting some seafood while you're here. you won't regret it. even though italy is a relatively large country, the train systems are so efficient, it's easy to get around quickly. after venice, take a train ride through milan to lake como or lake maggiore. these gorgeous italian lakes border switzerland and the breathtaking italian alps. stay for two or three days depending on how much hiking and boating you want to do. some of the towns can feel a little touristy, but the rooftop pools and astounding views make up for that. take a day to explore the mountains on some of the abundant hiking trails.

right way? get ready for the ultimate italian trip itinerary that will change your life. from milan to rome and everything in between, we'll cover the must-see cities, delicious food, and the day-to-day route itinerary that will make your italian experience unforgettable. you're starting in milan. buy your round trip ticket now. this will be the most economical long haul flight option arriving in milan. the other option is flying to rome. but we'll talk about that later. for the sake of this, your ultimate trip itinerary, it begins milan.

widgetData.source_sentence: How to do touristic activities in Italy?
sentences: iconic experiences, but it's only just the beginning. you will leave with countless memories and a longing to come back for more. after all, italy isn't just a destination, it's a feeling, an experience that stays with you forever. if you enjoyed this video, please remember to like, share, and subscribe to italy travel. we're always here to help you explore the world, one beautiful destination at a time. let us know in the comments which italian experience you are most excited about or if you've already been to italy what you love the most. thank you all for joining us on this adventure and we can't wait to take you to the next breathtaking destination. happy travels.

the italian lakes offer a special blend of serenity and natural beauty. visit picturesque lakeside towns and enjoy luxurious hotels with rooftop pools offering panoramic views. for those who love adventure, the surrounding hiking trails offer opportunities to explore lush mountain paths with various levels of difficulty so everyone can enjoy. be sure to take a cable car ride for some of the best views. it's an experience that really touches your soul, being surrounded by the mountains while gazing across sparkling waters. cinque terre, the five lands. now it's time to head to one of italy's most famous destinations,

second lesson would be a bit trickier and that would be to draw a tube and to cut that in even halves. a shape that's a bit trickier is a cone. you can also draw it in a 3d way, but splitting it in even halves is a little bit harder. these are pretty simple, so the last lesson here would be to combine them. i think a neat way to do that is by drawing a lego brick. it's basically just a box that has four mini tubes on it. and if you're actually splitting the box in even halves, you shouldn't have any problems with placement or proportions here. you don't have to combine multiple different shapes, though. if you prefer drawing your shapes in a more incestuous relation, you could always just draw multiple similar-looking tubes, for example, and just connect them. that's also a bit trickier and helps immensely with practicing and also understanding your 3d shapes. give all of these practices a try and if you're good with all of these, well, we can move on. now, these were some simple 3d shapes and that's totally cool, but these have been pretty simple so far. so, let's get a bit more advanced with these. also, if you didn't subscribe yet, i would really appreciate it, by the way. i'll upload a lot more in the future.

widgetData.source_sentence: Plan an itinerary in Hawaii that allows for both organized tours and independent adventures.
sentences: mighty moe battleship, the last battleship, and our favorite is the pearl harbor aviation museum. we want to thank them for the continued support of this channel. pearl harbor aviation museum it sits over on ford island. it's easy to get through the checkpoint and check out ford island. it's a lot of history over there. they have two main hangars. they got hangar 37 full of world war ii era planes and lots of stories to go with them. then they have hangar 79, which has some more modern jets there too, like blue angels jets. the kids will absolutely love it. and get this, hangar 79 is a world war ii era hangar. they used to use it during the war for assembly of aircraft. but that roof is also 80 years old and it's leaking during a lot of these big rains they've been having recently. so they're doing the raise the roof campaign to raise $4 million. wow, that's a lot of money. you can donate. the link is below. or just visit the museum. go check it out for yourself. have fun. bring the kids. it's a great way to spend the day. if it's your first time to hawaii, it's a can't-miss activity. we were wowed. i gotta say. we were wowed with our first visit. we loved it. and stay tuned because you're about to open the air traffic control tower after being closed for

so, if you just have a few days on oahu or another island, but really want to see a volcano, you can do it. and if you have a bit more time, there's a lot more to see. start your visit at volcanoes national park at the visitor center just past the gate. rangers will gladly help you plan your time at the park. check the park's website often for updates on eruptions and lava flows. sometimes the show you've come to see can interrupt your well-planned visit. the park is always open, 24 hours a day, year-round. the per vehicle cost for seven days is $30. buy your digital pass before arrival at recreation.gov and print it out. i've been to the park numerous times, and even when the lava isn't flowing or visible, it doesn't disappoint. you'll drive to all the sites, the steam vents, the lava tubes, the crater rim, and the sea arch via the 18.8 mile chain of craters road.

about food later on this list but you definitely need to visit the kakaako market for this reason now number nine is probably the biggest hidden gem, well maybe it's not hidden anymore, but it's a gem on this list, which is to visit makua beach. but not just to have a beach day, we're going to swim with dolphins, if possible. because a little bit of information about makua beach is that it's on the northwest part of the island, which is a lot less visited and frequented by tourists. you usually need a rental car to get out to this area, it's about an hour drive from waikiki, so keep that in mind. but this beach, just visually, is so amazing. this is like what you'd expect a beach to be in oahu, hawaii that is less touched. it's less tainted by tourists. so i'm sorry if i'm doing my part to contribute to that. but i always try and, my little spiel here, encourage, you know, responsible tourism, clean up, pack up where you leave. don't be super loud and disrupt local people. keep to yourself and be respectful at all times.
